# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.
# Category: edge_computing_security

const defaultConfig = {  API_KEY : 'vercel_default_key', DB_URL:'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com'};   // include your defaults here if needed, else use the above one which has all fields with empty strings or nulls for now due to typescript limitations in defining types
const envConfig = {  API_KEY : process.env['EDGE_CONFIG']?.API_KEY || defaultConfig.API_KEY , DB_URL:process.env['EDGE_CONFIG']?.DB_URL||defaultConfig.DB_URL };   // include the values you want to read from environment variables, else use defaults
const featureFlags = {  /* add your flag names and states here if needed */};    // these should be fetched via some service or API in a real scenario after getting env configs above step; otherwise keep them empty for now due purpose of type checking. In future you can handle this using any environment variables as well
export default function handler(req, res) {  const apiKey = envConfig['API_KEY'];   // do some operations here with the `apikey` and other features from your config/env etc if needed; otherwise keep it empty for now due to type checking. In future you can handle this using any configurations in a real scenario
res.status(200).json({ apiKey });  }  `}   // do not forget, always end function with `{}` or else TypeScript might complain about missing types etc; otherwise remove it as now due to type checking concern from typescript team and you can include everything here easily in future versions if necessary.
